title: Diosma eckloniana
taxonomy: Diosma eckloniana is classified as a species of flowering plant in the family Rutaceae. It is native to the Western Cape province of South Africa. Diosma eckloniana is a shrub or small tree that is often found in well-drained soils and rocky outcrops. The plant is also known as the 'Duke of York' due to its yellow flowers that resemble a royal crest. It is a member of the genus Diosma, which comprises several species of small, shrub-like plants.

intro: Diosma eckloniana, commonly known as Cape buchu, is a perennial, shrub-like plant native to the southwestern region of South Africa. Traditionally used in folk medicine, Cape buchu is valued for its potential health benefits, including its anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antiseptic properties, which may aid in treating digestive issues, respiratory problems, and skin conditions. The bioactive constituents of Diosma eckloniana include essential oils, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, such as ursolic and oleanolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. In traditional herbal medicine, Cape buchu is commonly prepared as a tea, tincture, or infusion, often used to treat various ailments, including indigestion, nausea, and fever.
